content.problemStatement:
Chef has two numbers XXX and YYY. Chef wants to find three integers A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC such that:


−1000≤A,B,C≤1000-1000 \le A, B, C \le 1000−1000≤A,B,C≤1000
mean([A,B,C])=Xmean([A, B, C]) = Xmean([A,B,C])=X
median([A,B,C])=Ymedian([A, B, C]) = Ymedian([A,B,C])=Y
Can you help Chef find such three integers?

As a reminder, mean([P,Q,R])=P+Q+R3mean([P, Q, R]) = \frac{P + Q + R}{3}mean([P,Q,R])=3P+Q+R​ and median([P,Q,R])median([P, Q, R])median([P,Q,R]) is the element at the 2nd2^{nd}2nd (middle) position after we sort [P,Q,R][P, Q, R][P,Q,R] in non-decreasing order.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the required mean and median of the three integers.


Output Format
For each test case, output three integers A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C which satisfy the given conditions.


It is guaranteed that an answer always exists under the given constraints.


If multiple answers exist, output any.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y – the required mean and median.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output three integers A, B, C (separated by spaces) such that -1000 ≤ A, B, C ≤ 1000, mean([A,B,C]) = X and median([A,B,C]) = Y. Print each triple on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each line we set A=Y, B=Y and C=3*X-2*Y. The triples satisfy the required mean and median.
C = 3*(-20) - 2*(-30) = -60 + 60 = 0? Wait compute: 3*(-20) = -60, -2*(-30)=60, C = 0. Actually output should be "-30 -30 0". Corrected below.
First case all zeros. Second case C = 3*(-50) - 2*20 = -150 - 40 = -190, median stays 20.

solution.approach: We fix the median element to be Y. Choose A = Y and B = Y. Then the third element C must satisfy (A + B + C) / 3 = X, i.e. C = 3*X - 2*Y. With this choice the sorted order is either [C, Y, Y] (if C < Y) or [Y, Y, C] (if C >= Y); in both cases the middle element is Y, so the median condition holds. Because X and Y are bounded by ±100, C = 3X - 2Y lies in [-500, 500], well inside the allowed range [-1000, 1000]. The construction works for every test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it))
        Y = int(next(it))
        C = 3*X - 2*Y
        out.append(f"{Y} {Y} {C}")
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
